text: Last class you said: in my view, the workshop was the best session.
Today you can say: if you rest, you will feel better.

If you rest, you will feel better.
If you take the medicine, you will get better.
If the fever does not go down, you should see a doctor.
You will feel dizzy if you stand up too fast.
Rubens, I feel awful. I have a fever, a sore throat, and a bad headache.
I am sorry to hear that. You should rest today. If you rest, you will feel better tomorrow.
I took a painkiller, but my whole body still aches. And I feel dizzy when I stand up.
That is the fever. If you drink water and sleep, the ache will get better. Do not stand up too fast.
Thank you. Should I see a doctor, or should I just wait?
If the fever does not go down by tomorrow, you should see a doctor. For now, rest and take sick leave.
I have a headache, a sore throat, and a slight fever.
If you rest, you will feel better tomorrow.
If you take the medicine on time, you will get better faster.
You should rest and take sick leave until you recover.

Hi Rubens, it is Sofia. I am sorry, but I cannot come to the morning session today. I do not feel well at all. I have a terrible headache and a sore throat, and I think I have a slight fever too. Last night I started to cough, and this morning I felt very dizzy when I got up. My whole body aches. I took a painkiller, but it did not help much. I am going to call a doctor later, and if she gives me a prescription, I will get the medicine right away. For now, I just want to rest. If I feel better tomorrow, I will join the afternoon panel. Thank you for understanding.

As a doctor, I give simple health advice every day, and I always use the same pattern. If a patient has a fever, I tell them to rest and drink water. If the pain does not get better in two days, I say they should see a doctor again. If you take the medicine on time, you will recover faster. I never frighten my patients. Instead, I explain what will happen if they follow the plan. For example, if you sleep well and avoid heavy meals, your body will heal more quickly. Good advice is clear and calm: tell the patient what to do, and what will happen if they do it.

Here is how I help a patient who describes a health problem. First, I ask where it hurts and how they feel. Then I listen carefully to all the symptoms. After that, I explain what is probably wrong in simple words. Next, I give clear advice and say what will happen if they follow it. Finally, I write a prescription if they need medicine.

I have a terrible headache and a sore throat.
If you feel too sick to work, you should take sick leave.
I have a headache, a sore throat, and a slight fever.
If you rest, you will feel better tomorrow.
You should rest and take sick leave until you recover.
If you take the medicine on time, you will get better faster.
